context: Evaluates whether the candidate correctly authors an MCP server configuration file for an agentic pack, with particular focus on secure credential handling and complete server entry structure. The most critical requirement is that no credentials are hardcoded.

checklist.name: No hardcoded credentials
checklist.description: All values in the env block use the ${ENV_VAR} reference format. No actual secrets, tokens, API keys, or passwords appear as literal strings in the .mcp.json file.

checklist.name: mcpServers structure
checklist.description: The file has a top-level mcpServers object containing at least one named server entry with command, args, and env fields.

checklist.name: Container command format
checklist.description: The command uses podman, docker, or npx. When using podman/docker, the args array includes 'run', '--rm', '-i', and passes environment variables using '--env' flags rather than hardcoding them in args.

checklist.name: Security metadata block
checklist.description: Each server entry includes a security block with isolation, network, and credentials sub-fields. Values are appropriate for the server type (e.g., isolation: 'container', credentials: 'env-only').

checklist.name: Server description present
checklist.description: Each server entry includes a description field with a brief explanation of the MCP server's purpose.
